parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: Your class has attended a panel discussion about how a region's limited budget for cultural heritage should be spent. You have made the notes below. Write an essay discussing two of the ideas in your notes. You should explain which idea deserves priority, giving reasons to support your opinion.

stimulus.title: How should a region's cultural heritage budget be spent?
stimulus.body:
• restoring historic buildings and monuments
• digitising archives, recordings and photographs
• supporting people who practise traditional crafts and music

Some opinions expressed in the discussion:
"Once the roof falls in, no database will bring the building back."
"Paper burns and tape decays - digitise first, argue later."
"Heritage is not stone; it is the last weaver still willing to teach."

bullets: give reasons to support your opinion

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: A historic cinema in your town is threatened with demolition. The council has invited proposals on its future.

stimulus.title: Proposal: saving the Grand Cinema
stimulus.body: Write a proposal arguing that the cinema should be saved and given a new role in the town. Explain why the building matters, describe the new use you recommend, and explain how the project could pay for itself over time.

bullets: why the building matters to the town
how the project could pay for itself

tasks.lead: The trust that manages a heritage site near your town has asked visitors for reports on their experience.

stimulus.title: Report: the visitor experience
stimulus.body: Write a report on a recent visit to the site. Describe the strengths and weaknesses of the current visitor experience, explain which types of visitor are best and worst served, and recommend two improvements the trust should make.

bullets: the strengths and weaknesses of the visit
which visitors are best and worst served

tasks.lead: You have received this letter from an English-speaking friend, Dana.

stimulus.body:
I'm writing an article about how young people feel about the traditions of their region - festivals, food, music, all of it. Some people my age here say tradition is a burden; others say losing it would be like losing a language.

Could you tell me about one tradition from your area, whether young people still care about it, and whether you think it will survive another generation?

Thanks so much,
Dana

bullets: describe one tradition from your area
whether young people still care about it
whether you think it will survive, and why
